metaTitle: The Boeing Atmosphere Global Documentation Hub - Patterns - Tables

header.title: Data table
header.description: Data tables are used to organize and display data efficiently across rows and columns.

sections.text:
Usage

Data tables should:

  • Keep information organized in a meaningful way, such as hierarchical or alphabetized
  • Allow user interaction so that a data display is customizable and interactive
  • Use with a logical structure that makes content easy to understand

sections.textBefore:
Alignment

For certain elements such as numerical values, percentages, etc., cells should be right-aligned to improve readability.
sections.textAfter:
1. Left-aligned
  2. Center-aligned
  3. Right-aligned

    4. Depending on the data, table cells can contain different types of content. For example, text, icons, checkboxes, tags, action buttons, and profiles.

sections.text:
Multiselect

Multi-select lets users select multiple rows in a data table to do bulk actions. When users select a checkbox, the multi-select actions bar appears above the table.

Placement

Always place checkboxes on the left side of the table. Always place the actions bar above the header row.

sections.textBefore:
Action bar and multiselect

The actions bar displays the number of selected items, icon buttons for bulk actions, and a close icon button. When users select the close icon, the selected rows are deselected and the table resets to its default state.
sections.textAfter:
1. Close icon button
  2. Number of selected rows
  3. Icon buttons for bulk actions

    4. The actions bar can contain many bulk actions. Actions can be hidden under a menu element to avoid overcrowding.

sections.textBefore: If only one action is possible, use a default button instead of only an icon.

sections.text:
Enabing multi-select

Users can trigger multi-select by clicking on any checkbox row. When at least one checkbox is selected, the header checkbox will switch to its indeterminate state. The actions bar will appear above the header row, displaying the number of selected elements.

Select/deselect all

Users can select and deselect all elements by manually checking and unchecking all of the row checkboxes. When all rows are selected the header checkbox will switch to its selected state.
Users can select and deselect all elements by checking the header checkbox.

sections.textBefore:
Disabled rows

Users can't select disabled rows. Disabled rows also can't receive focus.

Bulk actions

Users can perform bulk actions on the selected rows. After selecting rows, users can perform a bulk action by selecting a button in the actions bar.

Closing multi-select

After pressing the close button or after performing a bulk action, the data table will switch to its default state with all rows unselected.
